segments.text:
Classes of RNA
mRNA = messenger RNA
template for protein synthesis
rRNA = ribosomal RNA
RNA components of ribosomes, the organelles that perform protein synthesis
tRNA = transfer RNA
RNA “transporters” of amino acids for protein synthesis
other small RNAs
RNA component(s) of ribonuclear protein complexes
structural RNAs
regulatory RNAs

Eukaryotic RNA Polymerases
RNA polymerase I
most ribosomal RNAs  (5.8S, 18S, 28S)
RNA polymerase II
all mRNAs
many (?most/all?) regulatory RNAs
RNA polymerase III
all tRNAs
smallest (5S) ribosomal RNA
other small RNAs
A generic RNA Pol II transcribed gene

Minimal eukaryotic RNA Pol II promoter
many additional binding sites for transcriptional regulators
promoter  >  1 kb upstream of transcription start site

Regulatory factors influence of transcription initiation
(more on this in lecture 22)

TFIID = basal transcription initiation factor
identifies, binds to TATA box
begins displacing histones
TBP = TATA box Binding Protein
TFIIA binds to TFIID (not to DNA)

TFIIB  promoter “melting”
recruits, positions RNA Pol II
TFIIH = ATPase/ kinase
energy for strand separation

recognized
by TFIIB
recognized by TFIID
TFIID and TFIIB = key players

only once all 4 transcription factors are on DNA
histones removed
template DNA exposed
TFIIE
TFIIF
RNA Pol II with
TFIIE and TFIIF

A complete RNA Pol II basal transcription initiation complex
RNA Pol II will initiate transcription

switch from initiation to elongation
ser/thr kinase activity of TFIIH

carboxyterminal domain of RNA pol II (polymerase subunit)
tyr-ser-pro-thr-ser-pro-ser  (don’t learn sequence!)
positioned by interaction with TFIID
substrate for TFIIH kinase activity

altered active site
electrostatic repulsion
only TFIIE and TFIIF leave initiation complex with RNA Pol II
TFIIE = ATPase
TFIIF = helicase
TFIID/TFIIA/TFIIB/TFIIH dissociate or recruit additional RNA Pol

TFIID binds to the TATA box
via its TBP subunit
it’s not that simple!
TAFs = other subunits
of TFIID
other transcription factors
influence TFIID

a different way to arrange the same set of proteins
allows initiation complex assembly on promoters without TATA box

Distal regulatory elements
promoter – position and orientation dependent
position and orientation independent
